info.name: DebtFactory - Rule Engine V4 Multi-View
info.description:
Comprehensive test suite organized by user personas + vulnerability scenarios.

**Personas:** Salaried Employee, Self-Employed, NRI, Senior Citizen, Agriculture Worker
**V4 Regression:** Tests that will break after V4 BRE migration
**Vulnerability:** Type coercion, injection, contradictory state, extreme values, input validation
**Confidence:** Score integrity and near-miss detection

Total: 55+ scenarios across 14 folders.
Set {{baseUrl}} to your backend (default http://localhost:8000).

item.name: Persona: Salaried Employee
item.description: Standard salaried Pvt Ltd/Govt employee. Tests the strict-doc group (BOI/Indian Bank/IOB) per V4 CIBIL thresholds.

item.description: Validates the confidence_score = rules_passed/rules_total calculation is accurate.

item.name: CONF-01 Perfect applicant: all eligible banks should have confidence 1.0

exec: pm.test('evals sorted by confidence desc', () => { const s = b.evaluations.map(e => e.confidence_score); pm.expect(s).to.eql([...s].sort((a,c) => c - a)); });
pm.test('eligible flags consistent', () => { const f = b.evaluations.filter(e => e.eligible).map(e => e.bank_name).sort(); pm.expect(f).to.eql([...want].sort()); });

item.name: CONF-03 Near-miss: CIBIL 700 should show high confidence but not eligible

exec: pm.test('evals sorted by confidence desc', () => { const s = b.evaluations.map(e => e.confidence_score); pm.expect(s).to.eql([...s].sort((a,c) => c - a)); });
pm.test('eligible flags consistent', () => { const f = b.evaluations.filter(e => e.eligible).map(e => e.bank_name).sort(); pm.expect(f).to.eql([...want].sort()); });
// VULNERABILITY: A near-miss applicant with confidence 0.98 might be shown as
// 'almost eligible' in the UI. Ensure UI doesn't mislead users into thinking
// they can tweak one parameter and qualify.
